title: eupatorium sexangulare
intro: Eupatorium sexangulare, also known as Six-horned Snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the Eupatoriinae subfamily, characterized by its distinctive six-angled stems and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snakebites, due to its supposed ability to neutralize venom. The health benefits of Eupatorium sexangulare are believed to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil to create a topical remedy.
